The p95 network latency improved dramatically from 3935ms to 125ms (-97%), indicating the connection pooling and HTTP/2 successfully eliminated most network outliers.\n71\t\n72\t2. **Consistent Network Performance:** Post-optimization network latencies are tightly clustered between 113-129ms, with a p95 of only 125ms. This is a significant improvement over the baseline's highly variable network times.\n73\t\n74\t### What Didn't Work\n75\t\n76\t1. **Overall Latency Increased:** Despite network improvements, total router latency increased by 32% (median: 2317ms → 3060ms). This is the opposite of the targeted 20-30% reduction.\n77\t\n78\t2. **Inference Time Doubled:** Model inference time increased by 120% (median: 1338ms → 2944ms). This suggests either:\n79\t   - The model endpoint is under heavier load post-deployment\n80\t   - The model version changed\n81\t   - Different prompts or complexity\n82\t   - Measurement artifact (see below)\n83\t\n84\t3. **Target Missed:** The goal of 20-30% latency reduction was not achieved. Instead, we saw a 32% increase.\n85\t\n86\t---\n87\t\n88\t## Data Quality Issues\n89\t\n90\tThe post-optimization dataset contained 574 total records, of which:\n91\t\n92\t- **445 good records** (78%) - Valid timing measurements\n93\t- **129 broken records** (22%) - Corrupted timing data\n94\t\n95\tTiming corruption started at 09:28:42, where network time ≈ call time (99.9%) and inference time dropped to ~2ms. These records were excluded from analysis.\n96\t\n97\t---\n98\t\n99\t## Possible Causes for Latency Increase\n100\t\n101\t1. **Model Endpoint Load:** The ZAI proxy may be under heavier load during the post-optimization measurement period (08:02-09:28) compared to the baseline period (pre-05:35).\n102\t\n103\t2. **Model Version Change:** The model endpoint may have been upgraded or changed between baseline and post-optimization measurements.\n104\t\n105\t3. **Different Query Patterns:** The post-optimization period may have included more complex queries that require longer inference time.\n106\t\n107\t4. **Upstream Issues:** The ZAI proxy itself may have been experiencing performance issues unrelated to HTTP/2 or connection pooling.\n108\t\n109\t5. **Client-side Changes:** The optimization code may have introduced unintended side effects that increased overall latency despite network improvements.\n110\t\n111\t---\n112\t\n113\t## Router Overhead Breakdown (Post-Optimization)\n114\t\n115\tNon-ZAI router operations: prompt construction, JSON parsing, and intent processing.\n116\t\n117\t| Component | Median | p95 |\n118\t|-----------|--------|-----|\n119\t| Prompt Construction | 0.19ms | 0.22ms |\n120\t| JSON Parsing | 0.02ms | 0.04ms |\n121\t| Intent Processing | 0.02ms | 0.04ms |\n122\t\n123\tRouter overhead remains negligible (~0.23ms median), consistent with baseline.\n124\t\n125\t---\n126\t\n127\t## Recommendations\n128\t\n129\t1. **Investigate Inference Increase:** The 120% increase in inference time is the primary concern. This needs investigation at the ZAI proxy level to determine if model load, version, or configuration changed.\n130\t\n131\t2. **Separate Network from Model:** While network optimizations succeeded (dramatically reduced p95 network latency), the overall goal failed because model inference time dominates total latency. Future optimizations should focus on model-side improvements.\n132\t\n133\t3. **Cache Expansion:** Consider expanding the intent cache or implementing smarter cache keys to reduce overall call volume. Post-optimization measurements show **latency increased by 32%** rather than the targeted 20-30% reduction. The median router time increased from 2317ms to 3060ms.\n\n---\n\n## Deployment Timeline\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|------------|-------|\n| 05:02:45 | Intent router timing instrumentation verified (adc-2ksq1) |\n| 05:35:15 | **Baseline established** - 88 samples, median 2317ms (adc-2woc6) |\n| 05:49:02 | HTTP/2 support implemented |\n| 06:03:56 | Connection pooling implemented |\n| 08:02:49 | Post-optimization data collection begins |\n\n---\n\n## Comparison: Baseline vs Post-Optimization\n\n### Intent Router Total Time\n\n| Metric | Baseline | Post-Opt | Change |\n|--------|----------|----------|--------|\n| Count | 88 samples | 445 samples | +405 samples |\n| Median | 2317ms | 3060ms | **+32%** ❌ |\n| Average | 2634ms | 3321ms | **+26%** ❌ |\n| p95 | 5636ms | 6437ms | +14% |\n| p99 | 7808ms | 7937ms | +2% |\n\n### ZAI Proxy Call Time\n\n| Metric | Baseline | Post-Opt | Change |\n|--------|----------|----------|--------|\n| Median | 2317ms | 3060ms | **+32%** ❌ |\n| Average | 2634ms | 3321ms | **+26%** ❌ |\n| p95 | 5636ms | 6437ms | +14% |\n\n### Network Latency Component\n\n| Metric | Baseline | Post-Opt | Change |\n|--------|----------|----------|--------|\n| Median | 122ms | 117ms | **-4%** ✅ |\n| Average | 1186ms | 119ms | **-90%** ✅ |\n| p95 | 3935ms | 125ms | **-97%** ✅ |\n\n### Model Inference Time\n\n| Metric | Baseline | Post-Opt | Change |\n|--------|----------|----------|--------|\n| Median | 1338ms | 2944ms | **+120%** ❌ |\n| Average | 1449ms | 3201ms | **+121%** ❌ |\n| p95 | 4356ms | 6320ms | +45% |\n\n---\n\n## Key Findings\n\n### What Worked\n\n1. **Network Latency Improved:** Median network latency decreased from 122ms to 117ms (-4%), and average network latency dropped from 1186ms to 119ms (-90%). The p95 network latency improved dramatically from 3935ms to 125ms (-97%), indicating the connection pooling and HTTP/2 successfully eliminated most network outliers.\n\n2. **Consistent Network Performance:** Post-optimization network latencies are tightly clustered between 113-129ms, with a p95 of only 125ms. This is a significant improvement over the baseline's highly variable network times.\n\n### What Didn't Work\n\n1. **Overall Latency Increased:** Despite network improvements, total router latency increased by 32% (median: 2317ms → 3060ms). This is the opposite of the targeted 20-30% reduction.\n\n2. **Inference Time Doubled:** Model inference time increased by 120% (median: 1338ms → 2944ms). This suggests either:\n   - The model endpoint is under heavier load post-deployment\n   - The model version changed\n   - Different prompts or complexity\n   - Measurement artifact (see below)\n\n3. **Target Missed:** The goal of 20-30% latency reduction was not achieved. Instead, we saw a 32% increase.\n\n---\n\n## Data Quality Issues\n\nThe post-optimization dataset contained 574 total records, of which:\n\n- **445 good records** (78%) - Valid timing measurements\n- **129 broken records** (22%) - Corrupted timing data\n\nTiming corruption started at 09:28:42, where network time ≈ call time (99.9%) and inference time dropped to ~2ms. These records were excluded from analysis.\n\n---\n\n## Possible Causes for Latency Increase\n\n1. **Model Endpoint Load:** The ZAI proxy may be under heavier load during the post-optimization measurement period (08:02-09:28) compared to the baseline period (pre-05:35).\n\n2. **Model Version Change:** The model endpoint may have been upgraded or changed between baseline and post-optimization measurements.\n\n3. **Different Query Patterns:** The post-optimization period may have included more complex queries that require longer inference time.\n\n4. **Upstream Issues:** The ZAI proxy itself may have been experiencing performance issues unrelated to HTTP/2 or connection pooling.\n\n5. **Client-side Changes:** The optimization code may have introduced unintended side effects that increased overall latency despite network improvements.\n\n---\n\n## Router Overhead Breakdown (Post-Optimization)\n\nNon-ZAI router operations: prompt construction, JSON parsing, and intent processing.\n\n| Component | Median | p95 |\n|-----------|--------|-----|\n| Prompt Construction | 0.19ms | 0.22ms |\n| JSON Parsing | 0.02ms | 0.04ms |\n| Intent Processing | 0.02ms | 0.04ms |\n\nRouter overhead remains negligible (~0.23ms median), consistent with baseline.\n\n---\n\n## Recommendations\n\n1. **Investigate Inference Increase:** The 120% increase in inference time is the primary concern. This needs investigation at the ZAI proxy level to determine if model load, version, or configuration changed.\n\n2. **Separate Network from Model:** While network optimizations succeeded (dramatically reduced p95 network latency), the overall goal failed because model inference time dominates total latency. Future optimizations should focus on model-side improvements.\n\n3. **Cache Expansion:** Consider expanding the intent cache or implementing smarter cache keys to reduce overall call volume. The baseline showed a 12% cache hit rate; post-optimization cache statistics were not analyzed.\n\n4. **Timing Instrumentation Fix:** The timing corruption at 09:28:42 indicates a bug in the measurement code. This should be fixed before future measurements.\n\n5. **Consider Faster Model:** Given that inference now accounts for 96% of total latency (2944ms / 3060ms), consider using a faster model class (e.g., Haiku) or optimizing prompts to reduce processing time.\n\n---\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe HTTP/2 and connection pooling optimizations **successfully improved network latency** (median -4%, average -90%, p95 -97%), but **overall latency increased by 32%** due to a 120% increase in model inference time. ----------------------------------------------------\n40\tclass IntentCache:\n41\t    \"\"\"\n42\t    In-memory cache store for intent classifications with TTL support.\n43\t\n44\t    Storage format: {cache_key -> (intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp)}\n45\t    - cache_key: SHA256 hash of utterance + optional context\n46\t    - intent_mapping: list[IntentClassification] to return on cache hit\n47\t    - expiry_timestamp: Unix timestamp when entry expires (now + ttl_seconds)\n48\t\n49\t    Automatic cleanup:\n50\t    - Expired entries are removed on get() when cache size > 1000\n51\t    - Oldest entry is evicted when cache reaches capacity\n52\t\n53\t    Thread-safety: Not thread-safe - assumes single-threaded async execution.\n54\t    \"\"\"\n55\t\n56\t    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int minutes)\n62\t            max_size: Maximum number of entries before eviction (default: 1000)\n63\t        \"\"\"\n64\t        self.ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds\n65\t        self.max_size = max_size\n66\t        self._cache: dict[str, tuple[list, float]] = {}\n67\t        self._cache_hits = 0\n68\t        self._cache_misses = 0\n69\t        self._stats_log_interval = 50  # Log stats every N operations\n70\t\n71\t    def get(self, key: str) -> list | None:\n72\t        \"\"\"\n73\t        Retrieve cached intent mapping if exists and not expired.\n74\t\n75\t        Args:\n76\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n77\t\n78\t        Returns:\n79\t            Cached intent mapping if exists and not expired, None otherwise\n80\t        \"\"\"\n81\t        # Automatic cleanup: if self._cache:\n89\t            intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp = self._cache[key]\n90\t\n91\t            # Check if entry has expired\n92\t            if time.time() < expiry_timestamp:\n93\t                self._cache_hits += 1\n94\t                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n95\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n96\t                return intent_mapping\n97\t            else:\n98\t                # Remove expired entry\n99\t                del self._cache[key]\n100\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n101\t\n102\t        # Cache miss or expired\n103\t        self._cache_misses += 1\n104\t        return None\n105\t\n106\t    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n107\t        \"\"\"\n108\t        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n109\t\n110\t        Args:\n111\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n112\t            value: Intent mapping to cache Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n473\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n474\t\n475\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n476\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n477\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n478\t\n479\t            # Build user message\n480\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n481\t\n482\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n483\t\n484\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n485\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n486\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n487\t\n488\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n489\t\n490\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n491\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n492\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n493\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n494\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n495\t                user_message=user_message,\n496\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n497\t                max_tokens=80,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 96 to 80 - intent JSON is typically 40-70 tokens (single: ~40-50, multi: ~60-80)\n498\t                temperature=0.0,\n499\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n500\t            )\n501\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n502\t\n503\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n504\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n505\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n506\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n507\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n508\t\n509\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n510\t            raw_response = response\n511\t\n512\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n513\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n514\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n515\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n516\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n517\t\n518\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n519\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n520\t            try:\n521\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n522\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n523\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n524\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n525\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n526\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n527\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n528\t                raise e\n529\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n530\t\n531\t            # Measure classification processing time\n532\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n533\t            classifications = []\n534\t\n535\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n536\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n537\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n538\t                try:\n539\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n540\t                except ValueError:\n541\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n542\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n543\t\n544\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n545\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n546\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n547\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n548\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n549\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n550\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n551\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n552\t                )\n553\t                classifications.append(classification)\n554\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n555\t\n556\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n557\t\n558\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling at DEBUG level\n559\t            # Structured format: phase=duration_ms for easy parsing\n560\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n561\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n562\t            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n563\t            logger.debug(\n564\t                f\"router_timing phase=prompt_construction duration_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n565\t                f\"phase=proxy_call duration_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n566\t                f\"phase=proxy_network duration_ms={network_str} \"\n567\t                f\"phase=proxy_inference duration_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n568\t                f\"phase=json_parse duration_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n569\t                f\"phase=process duration_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n570\t                f\"phase=total duration_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n571\t                f\"intents_count={len(classifications)} {cache_stats}\"\n572\t            )\n573\t            logger.info(\n574\t                f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance ({total_ms:.0f}ms total)\"\n575\t            )\n576\t\n577\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n578\t\n579\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n580\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n581\t            timing_breakdown = {\n582\t                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n583\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n584\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n585\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n586\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n587\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n588\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n589\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n590\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n591\t            }\n592\t\n593\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n594\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n595\t\n596\t            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n597\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n598\t\n599\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n600\t\n601\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n602\t            \"\"\"\n603\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n604\t\n605\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n606\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n607\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n608\t            \"\"\"\n609\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n610\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n611\t\n612\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n613\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n614\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n615\t                retry_count += 1\n616\t                try:\n617\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n618\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n619\t                        utterance=utterance,\n620\t                        session_id=session_id,\n621\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n622\t                    )\n623\t                    return classifications, _\n624\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n625\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n626\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n627\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n628\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n629\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n630\t                    ) from retry_e\n631\t\n632\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n633\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n634\t                parse_error=str(e),\n635\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n636\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n637\t            ) from e\n638\t\n639\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n640\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n641\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n642\t\n643\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n644\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n645\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n646\t\n647\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n648\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n649\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n650\t\n651\t        except Exception as e:\n652\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n653\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n654\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n655\t\n656\t    async def route_utterance(\n657\t        self,\n658\t        utterance: str,\n659\t        utterance_id: str,\n660\t        session_id: str,\n661\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n662\t        \"\"\"\n663\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n664\t\n665\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n666\t\n667\t        Args:\n668\t            utterance: The user utterance\n669\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n670\t            session_id: Session ID\n671\t\n672\t        Returns:\n673\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n674\t\n675\t        Raises:\n676\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n677\t        \"\"\"\n678\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n679\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n680\t\n681\t        try:\n682\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n683\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n684\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n685\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n686\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n687\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n688\t\n689\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n690\t            try:\n691\t                store = await self._get_store()\n692\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n693\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n694\t            except Exception as e:\n695\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n696\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n697\t\n698\t            routed_intents = []\n699\t            for classification in classifications:\n700\t                # Create intent ID\n701\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n702\t\n703\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n704\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n705\t                    classification=classification,\n706\t                    session_id=session_id,\n707\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n708\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n709\t                )\n710\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n711\t\n712\t            return routed_intents\n713\t\n714\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n715\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n716\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n717\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n718\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n719\t                utterance=utterance,\n720\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n721\t                session_id=session_id,\n722\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n723\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n724\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n725\t            )\n726\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n727\t\n728\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n729\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n730\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n731\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n732\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n733\t                utterance=utterance,\n734\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n735\t                session_id=session_id,\n736\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n737\t            )\n738\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n739\t\n740\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n741\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n742\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n743\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n744\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n745\t                utterance=utterance,\n746\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n747\t                session_id=session_id,\n748\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n749\t            )\n750\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n751\t\n752\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n753\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n754\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n755\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n756\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n757\t                utterance=utterance,\n758\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n759\t                session_id=session_id,\n760\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n761\t            )\n762\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n763\t\n764\t    async def process_intent(\n765\t        self,\n766\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n767\t    ) -> dict:\n768\t        \"\"\"\n769\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n770\t\n771\t        Args:\n772\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n773\t\n774\t        Returns:\n775\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n776\t        \"\"\"\n777\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n778\t\n779\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n780\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n781\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n782\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n783\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n784\t        # the dispatch itself.\n785\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n786\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n787\t\n788\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n789\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n790\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n791\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n792\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n793\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n794\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n795\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n796\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n797\t            )\n798\t\n799\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n800\t            store = await self._get_store()\n801\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n802\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n803\t                \"resolved\",\n804\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n805\t            )\n806\t\n807\t            return {\n808\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n809\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n810\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n811\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n812\t            }\n813\t\n814\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n815\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n816\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n817\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n818\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n819\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n820\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n821\t            )\n822\t\n823\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n824\t            store = await self._get_store()\n825\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n826\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n827\t                \"resolved\",\n828\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n829\t            )\n830\t\n831\t            return {\n832\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n833\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n834\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n835\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n836\t            }\n837\t\n838\t        try:\n839\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n840\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n841\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n842\t            else:\n843\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n844\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n845\t        except Exception:\n846\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n847\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n848\t            raise\n849\t\n850\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n851\t        return result\n852\t\n853\t    async def _persist_timings(\n854\t        self,\n855\t        intent_id: str,\n856\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n857\t    ) -> None:\n858\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n859\t        try:\n860\t            store = await self._get_store()\n861\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n862\t        except Exception as e:\n863\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n864\t\n865\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n866\t        self,\n867\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n868\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n869\t    ) -> dict:\n870\t        \"\"\"\n871\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n872\t\n873\t        Args:\n874\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n875\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n876\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n877\t\n878\t        Returns:\n879\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n880\t        \"\"\"\n881\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n882\t\n883\t        logger.info(\n884\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n885\t            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n886\t        )\n887\t\n888\t        try:\n889\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n890\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n891\t                classification.intent_type,\n892\t                classification.lookup_kind\n893\t            )\n894\t\n895\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n896\t            from ..registry import get_project\n897\t            repo_path = None\n898\t            ssh_target = None\n899\t            host_alias = None\n900\t            registry = get_registry()\n901\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n902\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n903\t                if entry:\n904\t                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n905\t                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n906\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n907\t                    location = entry.display_path\n908\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n909\t                else:\n910\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n911\t\n912\t            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n913\t            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n914\t            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n915\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n916\t            project_cfg = (\n917\t                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n918\t                if classification.project_slug else None\n919\t            )\n920\t\n921\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n922\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n923\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n924\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n925\t                context=FetchContext(\n926\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n927\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n928\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n929\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n930\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n931\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n932\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n933\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n934\t                ),\n935\t            )\n936\t\n937\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n938\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n939\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n940\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n941\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n942\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n943\t            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n944\t\n945\t            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n946\t                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n947\t                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n948\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n949\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n950\t\n951\t                # Increment completed count\n952\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n953\t\n954\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n955\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n956\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n957\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n958\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n959\t                    total=total_sources,\n960\t                    source_name=source.value,\n961\t                    source_status=result.status,\n962\t                ))\n963\t\n964\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n965\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n966\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n967\t                timings.record(\n968\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n969\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n970\t                )\n971\t\n972\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n973\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n974\t                logger.error(\n975\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n976\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n977\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n978\t                )\n979\t\n980\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n981\t                failed_sources = []\n982\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n983\t                    failed_sources.append({\n984\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n985\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n986\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n987\t                    })\n988\t\n989\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n990\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n991\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n992\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n993\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n994\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n995\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n996\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n997\t                )\n998\t\n999\t                # Update intent status to failed\n1000\t                store = get_store()\n1001\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n1002\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n1003\t                    \"failed\",\n1004\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n1005\t                )\n1006\t\n1007\t                return {\n1008\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n1009\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n1010\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n1011\t                    \"error\": \"\"\"\n1420\t        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n1421\t\n1422\t        Args:\n1423\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n1424\t            timings:          \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n1524\t            }\n1525\t\n1526\t\n1527\t# Global router instance\n1528\t_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n1529\t\n1530\t\n1531\tdef intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import
